journal_entry.reason: Land and building purchased with fair value allocation ($6M land, $3M building), 50% paid by cheque plus 10% sales tax on building ($300K recoverable as ITC), 50% due in 6 months as payable.

output.reasoning: Text explicitly states "10% sales tax on the building" component. Land is tax-exempt. Tax applies only to the building portion of the purchase.
output.tax_context: 10% sales tax applies to the building component only; land is tax-exempt. The tax base is the allocated cost of the building (using the $9M purchase price allocated by fair value ratio), not the fair value itself. Tax is recoverable as Input Tax Credit.

asset_increase.reason: Land acquired at allocated fair value proportion of purchase price ($9M × 8/12 = $6M)
Building acquired at allocated fair value proportion of purchase price ($9M × 4/12 = $3M)
Input tax credit recoverable on building purchase (10% × $3M = $300K)

liability_increase.reason: Fifty percent of purchase price due in 6 months recorded as payable
Sales tax collected on building portion payable to tax authority

asset_decrease.reason: Cheque payment for balance of purchase price plus sales tax on building

ambiguities.aspect: Allocation of purchase price between land and building
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS requires allocation of lump-sum purchase price based on relative fair values — land FV $8M and building FV $4M are stated, so allocation is computable as 2/3 to land ($6M) and 1/3 to building ($3M).

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'paid by cheque' = cash payment at the stated amount.

ambiguities.aspect: Treatment of amount due in 6 months
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Amount due in 6 months = accounts payable (liability).
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Short-term payment plan (6 months) resolved at face value within normal credit terms.

ambiguities.aspect: Sales tax treatment on building portion
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Buyer-side tax is recoverable as Input Tax Credit — recorded as HST Receivable, not capitalized.
